name: Dominations
shortDescription: Dominations are an order of angels in Christian angelology, representing God's authority and justice. They mediate between God and lower angelic orders, ensuring divine will is executed.
description: In Christian angelology, Dominations (also known as Lordships in some traditions) constitute one of the nine orders of angels. They belong to the second sphere, often called the 'Middle Management' of the angelic hierarchy. Their primary function is to regulate the duties of lower angels, ensuring that God's divine will is carried out with precision and justice throughout the cosmos. Dominations are understood as embodiments of divine authority and leadership, reflecting God's own dominion over creation. They are believed to possess great wisdom and understanding, capable of comprehending complex divine plans and guiding others accordingly. Unlike angels directly involved in human affairs, Dominations primarily focus on the governance of the angelic realm itself. They are depicted as majestic and serene beings, radiating power and stability. Their presence is meant to inspire reverence and awe, reminding all of the supreme authority of God. The concept of Dominations contributes to the broader Christian understanding of a structured and purposeful universe, governed by divine intelligence and order. They represent a crucial link in the chain of command, ensuring that God's will is effectively implemented throughout creation.

sources.title: Dionysius the Areopagite: The Complete Works
sources.author: Pseudo-Dionysius
